name_en: Île de la Cité / Île Saint-Louis
name_local: Île de la Cité / Île Saint-Louis

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to get Berthillon ice cream
phrases.cultural_note: Berthillon at 31 Rue Saint-Louis en l'Île is Paris's most famous ice cream. The line can be long — nearby shops also sell Berthillon scoops with shorter waits. Try the salted caramel.

responses.english: Berthillon is on Île Saint-Louis, on Rue Saint-Louis-en-l'Île.
responses.phonetic: behr-tee-YOHN eh soor leel san LWEE, roo san lwee ahn LEEL
responses.french: Berthillon est sur l'Île Saint-Louis, rue Saint-Louis-en-l'Île.

phrases.cultural_note: Sainte-Chapelle has the finest Gothic stained glass in the world — 1,113 panels from the 13th century. Book timed tickets online. Visit on a sunny day for the most spectacular light.

responses.english: Sainte-Chapelle is inside the Palais de Justice complex.
responses.phonetic: lah sant shah-PEL eh dahn lahn-SANT doo pah-LEH duh zhoos-TEES
responses.french: La Sainte-Chapelle est dans l'enceinte du Palais de Justice.

phrases.text_local: Le marché aux fleurs est ouvert aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh mar-SHAY oh FLUHR eh oo-VEHR oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché aux Fleurs Reine Elizabeth II on Île de la Cité is open daily. On Sundays it becomes a bird market too. It's been here since 1808.

responses.english: The flower market is on Place Louis Lépine.
responses.phonetic: luh mar-SHAY oh fluhr eh soor lah plass lwee lay-PEEN
responses.french: Le marché aux fleurs est sur la Place Louis Lépine.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about walking along the Seine riverbanks
phrases.text_local: On peut se promener au bord de la Seine ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: ohn puh suh prohm-NAY oh BOR duh lah SEN ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: Walk down to the lower quays of Île Saint-Louis for a peaceful riverside stroll. In summer, the quays come alive with picnickers at sunset — grab wine and cheese and join in.

responses.english: Go down the stairs toward the quays, it is very pleasant.
responses.phonetic: duh-sahn-DAY layz es-kah-lee-YAY vehr lay kay, seh treh ah-gray-AH-bluh
responses.french: Descendez les escaliers vers les quais, c'est très agréable.

phrases.text_local: La Conciergerie, c'est là où Marie-Antoinette a été emprisonnée ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah kohn-syehr-zhuh-REE, seh LAH oo mah-REE ahn-twah-NET ah ay-TAY ahm-pree-zoh-NAY
phrases.cultural_note: Yes — the Conciergerie was the prison during the Revolution. Marie Antoinette's reconstructed cell can be visited. A combo ticket with Sainte-Chapelle saves money.

responses.english: The Conciergerie is right next door, on Quai de l'Horloge.
responses.phonetic: lah kohn-syehr-zhuh-REE eh zhoost ah koh-TAY, soor luh kay duh lor-LOZH
responses.french: La Conciergerie est juste à côté, sur le Quai de l'Horloge.
